content.problemStatement:
Suppose you have two arrays AAA and BBB, both of length NNN, and an integer parameter KKK.

Define the function score(A,B,K)\text{score}(A, B, K)score(A,B,K) as follows:


Initialize score=0\text{score} = 0score=0.
For each index iii from 111 to NNN in order:

If Ai+K<BiA_i + K \lt B_iAi​+K<Bi​, **swap** the arrays AAA and BBB (i.e., swap the entire array, not just the elements at iii).
Then, add AiA_iAi​ to score\text{score}score.


You're given the initial arrays AAA and BBB, and the integer parameter KKK. You need to handle QQQ updates. Each update is a **point update** on one of the arrays:


Type 111: Given integers ppp and xxx, set Ap=xA_p = xAp​=x.
Type 222: Given integers ppp and xxx, set Bp=xB_p = xBp​=x.
After each update, output the value of score(A,B,K)\text{score}(A, B, K)score(A,B,K).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the arrays AAA and BBB, and the integer parameter KKK, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array AAA.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ — the elements of the array BBB.
The fourth line contains a single integer QQQ — the number of updates.
The next QQQ lines each contain three space-separated integers tit_iti​, pip_ipi​, and xix_ixi​, describing the iii-th update as follows:

If ti=1t_i = 1ti​=1, set Api=xiA_{p_i} = x_iApi​​=xi​
If ti=2t_i = 2ti​=2, set Bpi=xiB_{p_i} = x_iBpi​​=xi​






Output Format
After each update, output on a new line the value of score(A,B,K)\text{score}(A,B,K)score(A,B,K).
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.
The sum of QQQ across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initially, we have A=[15,19,1,3,7]A = [15, 19, 1, 3, 7]A=[15,19,1,3,7] and B=[20,3,5,2,8]B = [20, 3, 5, 2, 8]B=[20,3,5,2,8].
The first update asks us to set A2A_2A2​ to 141414, after which A=[15,14,1,3,7]A = [15, 14, 1, 3, 7]A=[15,14,1,3,7].
The score is computed as follows:

i=1i = 1i=1: A1=14A_1 = 14A1​=14 and B1=20B_1 = 20B1​=20. A1+K<B1A_1 + K \lt B_1A1​+K<B1​, so we swap the arrays.
Now A=[20,3,5,2,8]A = [20, 3, 5, 2, 8]A=[20,3,5,2,8]. Add A1=20A_1 = 20A1​=20 to the score.
i=2i = 2i=2: A2=3A_2 = 3A2​=3 and B2=14B_2 = 14B2​=14. We swap the arrays.
Now A=[15,14,1,3,7]A = [15, 14, 1, 3, 7]A=[15,14,1,3,7]. Add A2=14A_2 = 14A2​=14 to the score.
i=3i = 3i=3: A3=1A_3 = 1A3​=1 and B3=5B_3 = 5B3​=5. We swap the arrays.
Now A=[20,3,5,2,8]A = [20, 3, 5, 2, 8]A=[20,3,5,2,8]. Add A3=5A_3 = 5A3​=5 to the score.
i=4i = 4i=4: A4=2A_4 = 2A4​=2 and B4=3B_4 = 3B4​=3. We don't swap the arrays.
AAA remains [20,3,5,2,8][20, 3, 5, 2, 8][20,3,5,2,8]. Add A4=2A_4 = 2A4​=2 to the score.
i=5i = 5i=5: A5=7A_5 = 7A5​=7 and B5=8B_5 = 8B5​=8. We don't swap the arrays.
AAA remains [20,3,5,2,8][20, 3, 5, 2, 8][20,3,5,2,8]. Add A5=8A_5 = 8A5​=8 to the score.

The final score is 20+14+5+2+8=4920+14+5+2+8 = 4920+14+5+2+8=49, which is the answer to the first query.
